title: Where Theres A Will Theres A Way 2

jokes.text: He says it's time for me to leave the nest, become independent...and have sex with girls.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining Will's advice about becoming independent
jokes.punchline: The innocent Carlton casually mentions having sex with girls as part of Will's guidance
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton innocence - delivers adult content with childlike earnestness. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: That's for not calling me after you left.
jokes.setup: Jackie slaps Will upon seeing him
jokes.punchline: Her casual explanation for the violence
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy setup with economical explanation

jokes.text: Since I didn't buy a program on the way in...could you please tell me what the heck is going on?
jokes.setup: Carlton witnessing the confusing Will/Jackie interaction
jokes.punchline: Treating the situation like a theatrical performance requiring a program
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor delivered with Carlton's characteristic formal confusion. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Man, look, that filly is from Philly. We grew up together.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking about Jackie's identity
jokes.punchline: Will's rhyming 'filly from Philly' description
jokes.explanation: Will's street poetry - economical rhyme that fits his character's verbal style

jokes.text: What do you want it to mean? / That she hates you and you have no interest in her.
jokes.setup: Will asking what Carlton wants 'grew up together' to mean
jokes.punchline: Carlton's brutally honest wish for their relationship status
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with Carlton's jealous honesty subverting expectation

jokes.text: I've seen a lot of girls smack you, Will. / Then I see the same girls leaving your room.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jackie slapping Will
jokes.punchline: Carlton's observation about Will's pattern with women
jokes.explanation: Carlton's naive observation about Will's romantic success despite conflicts

jokes.text: I'll fight you for her, Will. Any time, any place.
jokes.setup: Carlton seeing Jackie and being smitten
jokes.punchline: Carlton challenging Will to physical combat over a woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton absurdity - the preppy rich kid challenging Will to a fight

jokes.text: Relax. I got about as much chance with Jackie as you do.
jokes.setup: Carlton's challenge to fight for Jackie
jokes.punchline: Will deflating Carlton by comparing their equal lack of chances
jokes.explanation: Economical deflation of tension with realistic assessment

jokes.text: All right, y'all, everybody in rows one through 15...the party's in apartment 206.
jokes.setup: Tour bus pulls up to the apartment building
jokes.punchline: Jazz organizing tourists like a concert venue for Will and Carlton's apartment
jokes.explanation: Classic Jazz scheme - treating the apartment like a stadium. Good visual escalation.

jokes.text: And have money in your hands. Senior citizens get a 10-percent discount. I do take tips.
jokes.setup: Jazz organizing the party tour
jokes.punchline: Full commercial operation with discounts and tip requests
jokes.explanation: Jazz's entrepreneurial shamelessness escalates the absurdity

jokes.text: Or maybe we could book the Forum. I'm sure the Lakers will be on the road by then.
jokes.setup: Hilary planning her wedding extravaganza
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting booking a major sports arena for her wedding
jokes.explanation: Pure Hilary excess - treating a wedding like a major sporting event

jokes.text: I'm also Trevor's little girl.
jokes.setup: Sweet father-daughter moment about always being daddy's little girl
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately mentioning being Trevor's 'little girl' too
jokes.explanation: Hilary's innocent inappropriateness destroys the tender moment

jokes.text: No, no, no. Technically, you're his woman, not his little girl.
jokes.setup: Hilary calling herself Trevor's little girl
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's horrified correction
jokes.explanation: Uncle Phil's paternal horror at the inappropriate implication

jokes.text: Then why does he make me wear those outfits?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil correcting that she's Trevor's woman, not little girl
jokes.punchline: Hilary's innocent question about role-playing outfits
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Hilary's innocent question makes it worse. Very economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Excuse me. I'm scheduled for a heart attack.
jokes.setup: Hilary's inappropriate revelation about her relationship
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's formal announcement of his impending cardiac event
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly formal reaction to shocking news. Uncle Phil treating a heart attack like a calendar appointment is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Look out, Jerry. Tom's gonna get you.
jokes.setup: Will trying to wake up Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton talking in his sleep about Tom and Jerry cartoons
jokes.explanation: Carlton dreaming about cartoons fits his innocent character

jokes.text: This used to be my lawn. And this used to be my banister. And you used to be my tenants.
jokes.setup: Landlord discovering his property destroyed by the party
jokes.punchline: Building to the threat of eviction with parallel structure
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure with parallel 'used to be' phrases building to the punchline

jokes.text: He kicked your butt. / Excuse me, that's our butts, Carlton. We're in this together.
jokes.setup: Carlton gloating about the landlord's dominance over Will
jokes.punchline: Will correcting to plural 'butts' to remind Carlton they're both evicted
jokes.explanation: Simple grammatical correction that serves the plot point economically

jokes.text: Not anymore, Kemosabe.
jokes.setup: Will insisting they're in it together
jokes.punchline: Carlton using the Lone Ranger reference while abandoning Will
jokes.explanation: Carlton's pop culture reference while betraying the partnership

jokes.text: I don't have to make it on my own. I'm rich.
jokes.setup: Will arguing about proving independence
jokes.punchline: Carlton's matter-of-fact dismissal based on his wealth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton logic - wealth as the solution to all problems. High character integration.

jokes.text: I can be reached by fax.
jokes.setup: Carlton dramatically leaving Will behind
jokes.punchline: His formal, business-like exit instruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly formal Carlton exit line. Treating friendship dissolution like business correspondence.

jokes.text: You'll probably be there till you're in your 30s anyway. Like all those other serial killers.
jokes.setup: Will calling Carlton a baby for going home
jokes.punchline: Escalating to serial killer comparison for adult men living at home
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that plays on 90s stereotypes about adults living with parents

jokes.text: This is Jazz's mother. He's not here.
jokes.setup: Jazz answering his phone
jokes.punchline: Pretending to be his own mother to avoid creditors
jokes.explanation: Jazz's scheme to avoid responsibility fits his character

jokes.text: This is Publishers Clearinghouse. You've already won. / It's about time.
jokes.setup: Jazz still pretending on the phone
jokes.punchline: Will's eager response to fake sweepstakes news
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection with Will's desperate hope for good news

jokes.text: I heard somebody disguised as me ran a party scam.
jokes.setup: Will confronting Jazz about the party scam
jokes.punchline: Jazz claiming someone impersonated him to run the scam he actually ran
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jazz logic - claiming someone impersonated him to do the exact crime he committed. Perfect character-specific denial

jokes.text: Jazz, you got me kicked out my crib, man. / Let me make it up to you. / Talk fast, you've only got a few minutes to live.
jokes.setup: Jazz offering to make amends
jokes.punchline: Will's death threat with polite time limit
jokes.explanation: Will's controlled anger with courteous death threat timing

jokes.text: You can stay here with me. / In this roach motel?
jokes.setup: Jazz offering accommodation
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified reaction to Jazz's living conditions
jokes.explanation: Standard poor living conditions humor

jokes.text: Man, I was sneaking the bus back to the depot and I got held up. White dude. He took the bus too.
jokes.setup: Will demanding the party money from Jazz
jokes.punchline: Jazz claiming he was robbed and the thief stole the bus itself
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation - stealing an entire bus makes the lie more ridiculous

jokes.text: The money's gone, isn't it? / Like the Jheri curl.
jokes.setup: Will's resigned question about the money
jokes.punchline: Jazz comparing it to an outdated hairstyle trend
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference that dates the show but works as a 'completely gone' comparison

jokes.text: That's the Wilsons. You'll get to know them.
jokes.setup: Jazz showing Will around his awful apartment
jokes.punchline: Introducing Will to the cockroaches by family name
jokes.explanation: Standard poor living conditions humor with personified pests

jokes.text: Whoa, wait, back up, girl. Wax on, wax off.
jokes.setup: Jackie hitting Will again
jokes.punchline: Will using Karate Kid reference to stop her
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much twist or originality

jokes.text: That torch you've been carrying for me all these years...suddenly got a little too hot to handle.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why Jackie hit him
jokes.punchline: Metaphor about her torch burning too hot
jokes.explanation: Will's characteristic cockiness with extended metaphor

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey, don't you be calling me Lobe. I left that in Philly, where I thought I left you.
jokes.setup: Jackie calling Will by his old nickname 'Lobe'
jokes.punchline: Will's attempt at a comeback linking his nickname to leaving Jackie behind
jokes.explanation: Will's defensive response with attempted smooth comeback that doesn't quite land

jokes.text: You mean they're giving basketball scholarships to girls now? / Imagine my surprise. There I was just churning butter, and the phone rings.
jokes.setup: Will's sexist surprise about women's sports scholarships
jokes.punchline: Jackie's sarcastic response about primitive gender expectations
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that skewers Will's outdated thinking with vivid imagery. Well-earned sarcasm.

jokes.text: You mean they're giving basketball scholarships to girls now?
jokes.setup: Jackie explaining her basketball scholarship
jokes.punchline: Will's incredulous question about women's sports
jokes.explanation: Dated gender humor typical of the era - shows Will's ignorance but relies on stereotypes for the laugh

jokes.text: So beware of the bougieman.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Will living in Bel-Air
jokes.punchline: Jackie's pun combining 'bourgeois' and 'boogeyman'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that works on multiple levels - very quotable

jokes.text: I see this really bothers you. I'm gonna give you a couple of minutes.
jokes.setup: Tense moment between Will and Jackie about not calling
jokes.punchline: Will's awkward attempt to deflect serious emotion
jokes.explanation: Will's discomfort with real emotion showing through humor defense mechanism

jokes.text: Because I missed you too much. How's that? I said it. Happy? That what you wanna hear?
jokes.setup: Jackie pressing Will about why he didn't call
jokes.punchline: Will's emotional outburst admitting his feelings
jokes.explanation: Will's vulnerability breaking through his cool facade - good character moment

jokes.text: Don't hit your head on the rim.
jokes.setup: Will storming off after emotional admission
jokes.punchline: Jackie's basketball reference callback to earlier height discussion
jokes.explanation: Economical way to defuse tension while staying in character

jokes.text: Before long, you'd be dragging me down into the storeroom...and snatching all my gear off and everything.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why working together would be problematic
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy about Jackie's inevitable seduction of him
jokes.explanation: Will's ego-driven fantasy presented as concern - good character consistency

jokes.text: Then I have to nail you with a sexual-harassment suit...like the Anita Hill thing in reverse.
jokes.setup: Will's fantasy about Jackie seducing him
jokes.punchline: Topical reference to famous harassment case with gender reversal
jokes.explanation: Topical humor that dates the show but works for the character's logic

jokes.text: And it certainly does not include risking frostbite around you.
jokes.setup: Jackie explaining her professional focus
jokes.punchline: Temperature metaphor for not getting emotionally involved with Will
jokes.explanation: Cold/hot metaphor that works but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: Oh, Will. Wear something warm.
jokes.setup: After hiring Will for the job
jokes.punchline: Callback to the frostbite/temperature metaphors
jokes.explanation: Economical callback that maintains the temperature theme

jokes.text: Yo, man, I didn't really miss her. That was just game I was kicking there.
jokes.setup: Will talking to himself after emotional admission to Jackie
jokes.punchline: Immediately denying his genuine moment to maintain his image
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - can't admit vulnerability even to himself

jokes.text: Well, if it's gonna spoil.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil offering Carlton food while he packs
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical justification for taking food
jokes.explanation: Carlton's practical nature overriding his dramatic exit

jokes.text: Adversity is my oyster.
jokes.setup: Will boasting about overcoming challenges
jokes.punchline: Mangling the 'world is my oyster' expression
jokes.explanation: Will's confident malapropism is economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: This will go good with my adversity.
jokes.setup: Will taking food from Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Treating adversity as something to pair food with
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that extends the adversity wordplay

jokes.text: The last I saw him, he was in his room kissing the floor.
jokes.setup: Asked about Carlton's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dramatic reaction to being home
jokes.explanation: Carlton's over-dramatic gratitude visualized through literal floor-kissing

jokes.text: There's no place like home.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil welcoming Will back anytime
jokes.punchline: Will's ironic Wizard of Oz reference while refusing to come home
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference without much added wit

jokes.text: Please, whatever happened to getting down on one knee? / Daddy, that's for old fogies. / I got down on one knee. / Sustained.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil complaining about modern proposals vs traditional ones
jokes.punchline: Legal 'sustained' objection to being called an old fogie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Phil moment - using legal terminology in family conversation. Well-structured escalation.

jokes.text: I ain't no bungee expert or nothing...but I don't think he's supposed to be slamming into the ground like that.
jokes.setup: Watching Trevor's bungee jump proposal go wrong
jokes.punchline: Will's understatement about obvious disaster
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart observation about the obvious disaster with characteristic understatement

jokes.text: Trevor didn't look so bad for a dude that had a concrete face-lift.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Trevor's funeral
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of fatal impact with ground
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about death that's characteristic for Will but somewhat cheap

jokes.text: Fool. Like that was his real head.
jokes.setup: Will's comment about Trevor's appearance
jokes.punchline: Jazz's even darker implication about the damage
jokes.explanation: Jazz escalating the dark humor but crossing into tasteless territory

jokes.text: No, I mean technically. Am I a widow or what?
jokes.setup: Family consoling Hilary about Trevor's death
jokes.punchline: Hilary's practical concern about her legal status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - focusing on technicalities during grief. Great character integration.

jokes.text: If I hadn't asked for a special proposal...Trevor would be in my arms...instead of in my purse.
jokes.setup: Hilary blaming herself for Trevor's death
jokes.punchline: Implication that Trevor's remains are literally in her purse
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation with perfect structure - 'in my arms' vs 'in my purse' creates strong contrast

jokes.text: Hilary...Trevor wasn't cremated.
jokes.setup: Hilary carrying around what she thinks are Trevor's ashes
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv's horrified correction about cremation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the next joke perfectly - straight-man delivery that makes Hilary's mistake clear

jokes.text: Then who's this?
jokes.setup: Learning Trevor wasn't cremated while holding an urn
jokes.punchline: Hilary's confusion about whose ashes she's been carrying
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline - economical and creates horrifying implications. Strong structure.

jokes.text: Is he single?
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv suggesting Hilary seek spiritual comfort from Reverend Boyd
jokes.punchline: Hilary's romantic interest in the reverend
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary misdirection - turning spiritual advice into dating opportunity

jokes.text: Right. He's up there with my little dog Scruffy.
jokes.setup: Being told Trevor is in heaven
jokes.punchline: Hilary comparing Trevor to her dead pet
jokes.explanation: Hilary's innocent equation of fiancé and pet in afterlife

jokes.text: Yeah, and the bus he rode in under.
jokes.setup: Discussion of dead pets in heaven
jokes.punchline: Will's dark reference to pets being hit by buses
jokes.explanation: Will's dark humor about pet death - somewhat cheap and tasteless

jokes.text: Must everything be a joke to you? My sister's in a fragile condition. I'm trying to help her bounce back. / Bounce. Trevor bounced.
jokes.setup: Carlton criticizing Will's inappropriate humor
jokes.punchline: Will connecting 'bounce back' to Trevor's bungee accident
jokes.explanation: Will can't resist the wordplay even when being scolded - true to character but somewhat cheap

jokes.text: Son, go get yourself a milk.
jokes.setup: Will making inappropriate death jokes
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil treating Will like a child who needs timeout
jokes.explanation: Uncle Phil's paternal dismissal is perfect - treating Will like a misbehaving child

jokes.text: Every inch of that newsroom reminds me of Trevor. His desk, his makeup bib...the shower in his office.
jokes.setup: Hilary explaining why she can't return to work
jokes.punchline: Increasingly personal items culminating in office shower
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure revealing Trevor's workplace privileges/behavior

jokes.text: Go to the mall with my father? Sorry, I forgot.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil offering to take Hilary shopping to cheer her up
jokes.punchline: Hilary's horror at the social impossibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary vanity - being seen shopping with dad is socially unacceptable

jokes.text: So with that in mind, what are you doing later?
jokes.setup: Jazz giving philosophical advice about living each day to the fullest
jokes.punchline: Immediately hitting on the grieving widow
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz move - using tragedy advice as pickup opportunity. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I forgot. There's a hostage situation back at my building. SWAT team said it'd be a week before we could get back in.
jokes.setup: Jazz needing to leave after being rejected
jokes.punchline: Elaborate excuse about why he can't go home
jokes.explanation: Jazz's creative excuse-making to avoid leaving - fits his scheming character

jokes.text: Forgive me for what I'm about to do.
jokes.setup: Will preparing to manipulate Hilary into giving up the pool house
jokes.punchline: His formal apology before psychological manipulation
jokes.explanation: Will's acknowledgment of his own manipulation sets up the scheme well

jokes.text: Of course, there is the pool house. Never mind, forget about that, though.
jokes.setup: Will agreeing Hilary should avoid reminders of Trevor
jokes.punchline: Planting the pool house idea while pretending to dismiss it
jokes.explanation: Will's reverse psychology begins - good manipulation technique shown comedically

jokes.text: I bet if those walls could talk...they'd whisper: 'Trevor.'
jokes.setup: Will describing the romantic pool house memories
jokes.punchline: The walls whispering Trevor's name like a romantic movie
jokes.explanation: Will's melodramatic manipulation continues the psychological scheme

jokes.text: I was thinking maybe I could turn that pool house into a law library. / Stop playing, listen. / A greenhouse. That would work, huh?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil planning to repurpose the pool house
jokes.punchline: Multiple plans while ignoring Will's protests
jokes.explanation: Uncle Phil's obliviousness to Will's hints while making his own plans

jokes.text: Check it out. It's important that I have my privacy. Say a honey be tiptoeing up my crib. That's my business, not yours.
jokes.setup: Will negotiating pool house rules
jokes.punchline: Immediately establishing need for romantic privacy
jokes.explanation: Will's priorities immediately turn to romantic opportunities - true to character

jokes.text: Fine. No skinny-dipping. / You gotta promise me that same thing.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil setting pool rules
jokes.punchline: Will's request creates horrifying mental image
jokes.explanation: Will's comeback creates unwanted mental image of Uncle Phil skinny-dipping

jokes.text: How come he gets the pool house...and I'm stuck upstairs with the grieving widow?
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about living arrangements
jokes.punchline: Describing his sister as 'the grieving widow' like a burden
jokes.explanation: Carlton's selfish view of his grieving sister as an inconvenience

jokes.text: It's my property. I'm gonna inherit it after the big guy takes his bungee jump.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming ownership of the property
jokes.punchline: Jazz's casual reference to Uncle Phil's future death via bungee jumping
jokes.explanation: Jazz connecting Uncle Phil to Trevor's fate - dark but somewhat predictable callback

jokes.text: Okay, tallest one gets the bedroom. I need the big closet for my peacock suit.
jokes.setup: Carlton and Will negotiating room arrangements
jokes.punchline: Carlton's mention of owning a peacock suit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton detail - of course he owns a peacock suit. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Will! So glad it's you. I heard someone disguised as me just ran a party scam. / I'm sorry, what? / How many people think we doing that again?
jokes.setup: Jazz appearing at the door with the same party scam line
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about repeating the same scam/episode structure
jokes.explanation: Interesting meta-joke about repeating content, though execution is somewhat clunky
